questions.question_text: What is the ultimate goal of the OHS Act?

options.b: To ensure workers' safety and health

questions.question_text: What does OSHA stand for?
What is considered a worker's basic right under the OHS Act?

options.b: A workplace free from recognized hazards

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of a hazard?
How does ALARP apply to management?

options.a: It ensures that risks are eliminated entirely
options.b: It reduces risks to a practicable minimum level.
options.c: It guarantees financial compensation for hazards.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a factor in defining risk?
What is the specific responsibility of workers under the OHS Act?

options.a: Reporting safety hazards to the employer

questions.question_text: Employers must provide training for employees when:
What is a worker's primary responsibility regarding PPE?
Which of the following is NOT a type of OSHA violation?
Why might OSHA conduct an unannounced inspection?

options.a: To ensure compliance with reporting rules
options.c: As a routine check in high-risk industries

questions.question_text: What is the primary purpose of fire extinguishers?

options.a: Control large fires in industrial settings

questions.question_text: What is the final stage of fire development?
Which fire class involves metals such as magnesium and sodium?
Which of the following is considered a form of PPE?
If a worker observes an unsafe condition, their first action should be to:

options.a: Inform their supervisor or safety team
options.c: File a report directly with OSHA
options.d: Wait for another worker to report it

questions.question_text: What is the key principle of the Stop-Call-Wait rule?

options.a: Employees must stop unsafe work and inform the supervisor.
options.b: Employees should report hazards after completing tasks.
options.c: Supervisors must stop work until an inspection occurs.
options.d: Workers must wait for written employer instructions.

questions.question_text: During an OSHA inspection, workers may:

options.b: Join the inspector and share concerns
options.c: Avoid wearing PPE during the inspection

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a key component of the fire tetrahedron?
What is the primary lesson from major industrial disasters?

options.a: Proper enforcement of safety standards can prevent accidents.
options.b: Hazards in high-risk industries are unavoidable.
options.c: Training programs should only focus on senior workers.
options.d: PPE eliminates the need for regular safety inspections.

questions.question_text: What is the best way of dealing with a hazard to ensure others are not put at risk?

options.b: Place a barrier tape around it
options.c: Display a notice or warning sign
options.d: Leave it for others to sort out

questions.question_text: Which of the following definition is "the exposure limit values"?

options.a: maximum value of the C-weighting sound pressure in pascals (Pa) to which a person is exposed during the working day
options.b: daily equivalent A-weighted sound level, expressed in dB (A)
options.c: the levels of noise above which employees may not be exposed
options.d: the noise exposure levels at which certain actions are required

questions.question_text: Which of the following definition is "the exposure action values"?

options.a: maximum value of the C-weighting sound pressure in pascals (Pa) to which a person is exposed during the working day
options.b: daily equivalent A-weighted sound level, expressed in dB (A)
options.c: the levels of noise above which employees may not be exposed
options.d: the noise exposure levels at which certain actions are required

questions.question_text: Which of the following is wrong according to the Control of Noise at Work Regulation?

options.a: If exposure cannot be reduced by other means, and is likely to above the upper EAV, then ear protection must be provided by the employer and used by employees
options.b: Health surveillance is required if the risk assessment indicates that there is a risk to health from noise (those regularly exposed above the upper EAV) without taking account of noise reduction from use of hearing protectors
options.c: Information, instruction, and training must not be provided for those exposed at or the lower EAV
options.d: A formal risk assessment is necessary at or above the lower EAV

questions.question_text: Which of the following is wrong according to the risk assesment to control noise at work?

options.a: Systematically identify noise sources having high sound pressure
options.c: For those exposed to noise determine daily exposure pattern and exposure duration
options.d: Estimate likely exposure (daily and peak) to noise and compare with limit and action values.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is wrong according to the risk management to control noise at work?

options.a: Review the following; actual work practices, exposure assessments, health surveillance data, finding from workplace inspections, individual complaints related to noise exposure and new ways to reduce risk
options.b: Provide health surveillance (audiometry) only for those above 45 years old
options.c: Give employees information and training on safe work practices and hearing protection
options.d: Maintain noise control equipment and hearing protection

questions.question_text: Which of the following is one of the common sources of hand-transmitted vibration?
Which of the following is one of the common sources of whole body vibration?
Which of the following is wrong according to the potential health effects of whole body vibration?

options.a: The best recognized effects are on the lumbar spine—non-specific low-back pain (LBP), sciatica, lumbar disc degeneration.
options.b: A systematic review by NIOSH (1997) described evidence on the association with LBP as 'strong' (15 of 19 studies positive), but there is less certainty about the dose-response relationship.
options.c: Other suggested effects include: neck pain and cervical disc degeneration; autonomic disturbance; and disorders of balance and digestion.

questions.question_text: What is the main purpose of Occupational Health and Safety (OHS)?

options.B: To save lives, prevent injuries, and protect worker health
options.C: To enforce legal standards on employers
options.D: To limit the use of hazardous materials

questions.question_text: What is a fire drill?

options.C: A training exercise to practice safe evacuation
options.D: A fire alarm sound test only

questions.question_text: According to the OSH Act, who is primarily responsible for workplace safety?
Which of the following best defines a "hazard"?

options.B: Something that may cause harm or damage

questions.question_text: What is the correct formula for calculating risk?
What is a “tolerable risk”?

options.A: A risk that has no impact
options.B: A risk level that can be accepted
options.C: A risk that only affects property
options.E: A risk caused only by machines

questions.question_text: What is the first step when using a fire extinguisher according to PASS method?
What does the acronym ALARP stand for?

options.A: All Levels Are Regulated and Protected

questions.question_text: Which fire class includes cooking oils and fats?
What should workers do in case of unsafe situations or conditions?

options.E: Ask a co-worker for help quietly

questions.question_text: According to the OSH Act, who is primarily responsible for workplace safety?
What should a worker do if they discover a fire?

options.D: Raise the alarm and evacuate safely

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a worker right under the OSH Act?
What is the main cause of fatalities during fires?

options.A: They are used only by professional firefighters
options.B: All types are safe to use on electrical fires
options.C: They are considered first-aid firefighting equipment
options.E: They can be thrown to extinguish flames

questions.question_text: Which class of fire involves flammable gases like propane and butane?
What does the fire extinguisher acronym “PASS” stand for?
Who is responsible for providing Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)?
What is the main difference between hazard and risk?

options.A: Hazard is a possibility; risk is a legal issue
options.B: Hazard causes damage; risk measures the chance of damage
options.C: Hazard is invisible; risk is visible
options.D: Hazard is a worker; risk is an employer
options.E: Hazard is about paperwork; risk is about fire

questions.question_text: Which body parts are included in Group A of the REBA scoring system?

options.b: Upper arms, lower arms, wrist, and coupling
options.c: Neck, shoulders, upper arms, and hands

questions.question_text: How is the final REBA score calculated?

options.a: By summing up individual body part scores directly
options.b: By multiplying the Group A and Group B scores
options.c: By combining Scores A, B, C, and the Activity Score

questions.question_text: Which of the following topics is NOT directly addressed by ergonomics?
Who is primarily responsible for ensuring workplace safety?
Which of the following is a result of poor organizational ergonomics?
What is tolerable risk?

options.a: Any risk that is not fatal
options.b: A hazard that cannot be controlled
options.d: Risk below a defined acceptable level

questions.question_text: What is the primary goal of ergonomics?

options.a: To increase costs in workplace design
options.b: To optimize human wellbeing and system performance
options.c: To reduce the need for teamwork
options.d: To make tasks more difficult for workers

questions.question_text: Which biological hazard group is unlikely to cause human disease?
Which fire class includes kitchen fires?
Organizational ergonomics aims to optimize:

options.c: Socio-technical systems, including work design and teamwork
options.d: The design of tools and equipment

questions.question_text: What is ALARP?
What rights do workers have during OSHA inspections?

options.b: Halt all work until inspections are complete

questions.question_text: What does the OHS Act require employers to do?

options.b: Provide safe working conditions free of recognized hazards

options.b: Evaluating the risks which cannot be avoided
options.d: Giving individual protective measures priority over collective protective measures.

options.b: Halt all work until inspections are complete

questions.question_text: What is a key element of the fire triangle?

What method of heat transfer occurs through electromagnetic waves?
What should you do if you discover a fire?

options.b: Raise the alarm and fight the fire if safe

options.a: Train workers in fire suppression techniques
